title: Sodium ortho-phenylphenate - Canada.ca
content:
## Sodium ortho-phenylphenate
- Sodium ortho-phenylphenate
This substance was identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP).
- Summary of publications
- Follow-up activities

## Summary of publications
Common name and acronym
Public comments on the draft screening assessment
Final screening assessment
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Sodium ortho-phenylphenate (SOPP)
[1,1'-Biphenyl]-2-ol, sodium salt
Significant New Activity (SNAc) provisions

## Follow-up activities
Although SOPP is not considered to be harmful at levels of exposure considered in the assessment, it is considered to have a health effect of concern based on laboratory studies. Therefore, there may be a potential risk if exposures were to increase. The proposed follow-up activity is to apply the SNAc provisions of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA 1999) to SOPP.
Publication in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 156, No. 47 of a notice of intent to amend the Domestic Substances List (DSL) to apply the SNAc provisions of CEPA 1999 and start of a 60-day public comment period.
Publication of the final screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 156, No. 47 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment (including follow-up activities). The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 154, No. 9 .
